code:
proof fn phi_2_free_pcid_pagetable_is_empty(mm: MemoryManager, pcid: Pcid)
    requires
        mm.pagetables_wf(),
        0 <= pcid < PCID_MAX,
        mm.get_free_pcids_as_set().contains(pcid),
    ensures
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().mapping_4k() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().mapping_2m() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().mapping_1g() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Free PCIDs have is_empty() page tables, but is_empty only constrains user-space L4 entries (kernel_l4_end..512) — kernel entries below kernel_l4_end could still hold mappings while the table is considered "empty"

proof fn phi_3_iommu_kernel_l4_end_zero(mm: MemoryManager, ioid: IOid)
    requires
        mm.iommutables_wf(),
        0 <= ioid < IOID_MAX,
    ensures
        mm.iommu_tables@[ioid as int].unwrap().kernel_l4_end == 0,
{
}
reason: IOMMU tables have kernel_l4_end == 0, meaning is_empty checks ALL 512 L4 entries — but this also means IOMMU tables have no kernel region at all, so a device could potentially map the entire 512-entry address space with no kernel reservation

proof fn phi_5_pagetable_iommu_page_closure_disjoint(mm: MemoryManager, pcid_i: Pcid, pcid_j: Pcid)
    requires
        mm.pagetables_wf(),
        0 <= pcid_i < PCID_MAX,
        0 <= pcid_j < PCID_MAX,
        pcid_i != pcid_j,
    ensures
        mm.page_tables@[pcid_i as int].unwrap().page_closure().disjoint(
            mm.page_tables@[pcid_j as int].unwrap().page_closure()),
{
}
reason: Page table page closures are pairwise disjoint across ALL pcids including free ones — free pcids with is_empty tables still have cr3 in their l4_table dom, so if two free pcids share a cr3 pointer this would be violated, suggesting the spec silently assumes unique cr3 allocation
